summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Learning & Organization Development professional who thrives on designing educational programs grounded in evidence, solving problems through data analysis, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve learning experiences—through human-centered, systematic approaches.

bullets.text: Facilitate {coord|training} webinars for 600+ participants, enhancing product understanding and driving a 78% client conversion rate
Develop guides and workflows for 70+ cross-functional {cross|staff}, improving {data|staff knowledge} and reducing {prog|onboarding} time by 75%
Conduct needs assessments with 25+ global stakeholders to identify {data|training needs} and design 3 new educational programs
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to launch 15+ educational products, refining program positioning, delivery strategy, and operations via feedback analysis
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming feedback data into actionable reports that informed leadership strategies
Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences, communicating insights to C-suite stakeholders to guide decisions on training priorities
Managed SharePoint dashboards tracking people metrics, survey insights, and engagement data to drive employee recognition
Improved employee experience via 5 cross-functional projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Streamlined onboarding across 3 global locations by coordinating process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
Managed organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing cross-functional stakeholders
Initiated a {ex|DEI} pilot with an {coord|Inclusive Leadership workshop} for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 other global locations
Strengthened engagement via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Designed and {coord|facilitated} a 3-week English immersion {prog|curriculum} for 12 students, using feedback to improve {data|learning outcomes}
Delivered culturally responsive lessons, adjusting content and pacing in real time to meet diverse {ex|learner needs}
Developed {data|instructional materials} aligned to learning objectives to support knowledge retention and skill application

bullets: Relevant Coursework: Organizational Psychology, Research Methods, Group Dynamics & Leadership, Consumer Psychology
